channel.title: Your friendly neighborhood demon
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Your friendly neighborhood demon" - A completely incompetent demon is summoned by a researcher

item.title: Re: Your friendly neighborhood demon
item.content:encoded:
I feel like you've definitely got a fun setting for a story here; but I don't really have a strong feeling for what you're actually like, doing here? Specifically: it seems like you're spending a lot of time interviewing the SCP and like showing off how amusing they are but my concern is that this doesn't really like… go anywhere? There's no big emotional or narrative arc within these interviews and so you're left kinda relying on them being interesting enough alone to carry this and…I'm not sure they will be?

There's definitely elements I like e.g. the exploration of the like, kinda paranoia you get working at the Foundation if they're willing to kill you for breaking a rule; and the whole angle of the demon being so annoying hell is glad they're gone; and I just…kinda want to see you tell more of a complete story around these and really use these elements to…do something?

item.title: Re: Your friendly neighborhood demon
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for reading my idea Dr Langley!
I'm glad that you like it. If you come up with any kind of advice I'll be really happy to hear it!

item.title: Forum Criticism Team - Captain Post
item.content:encoded:
Dr Langley, when providing concept feedback to authors in the forums, please try to make sure your critique is specific to the material at hand rather than statements that could be applied to any concept interchangeably.

For some guidelines on giving feedback, you can look at this thread: http://scp-wiki.wikidot.com/forum/t-9804362/so-you-want-to-be-a-concept-reviewer

item.title: Re: Your friendly neighborhood demon
item.content:encoded: This sounds like it would make for a fun read as a main site article. There's not much I can really add other than this is a great idea IMO. To get some more professional critique and potential greenlights, shoot a member over at the butterfly squad a PM.

item.title: Your friendly neighborhood demon
item.content:encoded:
A completely incompetent demon is summoned by a researcher. The researcher gets terminated and the demon tries (miserably) to do its job.

I moved and trimmed your thread summary, since it was taking up extra space in the main forum screen. Try to keep thread descriptions (the text in the little box under the thread title) and titles short so you don't crowd out other authors in the forum listing. ~Zyn

Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre: Mostly comedy, secondly psychological.

Page Layout:

+ show block
– hide block

Class and ConProcs before and after the “incident”. Description of the Book and, adding the “as of dd/mm/yyyy” the description of the Entity. At least three Interview logs with the entity (or just one log with multiple “extracts” to give the idea). An example of a contract the entity would propose (with a lot of Latin and high-sounding words of jurisprudence to give it an overly serious characterization to contrast the demon’s one) and, finally, a note from Hell itself.

Elevator Pitch:

+ show block
– hide block

SCP will be composed of two parts:
• A book, which the Foundation seized, that describes various thaumaturgical/satanical rituals. One of these being the summoning of demons.
• A weak and lower demon, summoned by a Researcher trying to bargain for money, power, etc. The demon is rather “stupid”. It’s forcibly stuck in the room he was summoned in due to the blood glyph drawn on the floor and his only goal is to acquire as many souls as he can.

Central Narrative:

+ show block
– hide block

The demon acts all high and mighty, like a stereotypical devil from fantasy literature, talking big and trying to sway people into making contracts with it. It is, however, extremely weak and incompetent. Gets easily irritated, lies terribly, and doesn’t seem to be able to trick anyone into doing anything evil. Quite on the contrary it seems that people behave better around him, much to its irritation.
Meanwhile through the interview logs the reader experiences the reaction to the loss of the researcher that summoned it, that was terminated for his abuse. The conversations with the demon will parallel a possible set of reactions to the Foundation methods in dealing with rule breakers.
In the end a note will be found in the site director’s office thanking the Foundation for containing the demon without exorcising him back, humorously hinting that the demon was so incompetent that he was disliked in Hell.

Hook/Attention-Grabber:

+ show block
– hide block

Most of the article features a comedic style derived from the stereotypical “big demon” behavior being attributed to such a wimpy and incapable creature, its inefficient methods and, lastly his frustration about it.
On the other side readers will delve into some possible reactions to the idea that someone you worked with every day, maybe even dated or considered a friend, has been literally killed for breaking the rules at work. Even if working at the Foundation they are still human beings after all.

Additional Notes:

+ show block
– hide block

Hiding behind a copious humorous screen there will be a cynical reasoning about the Foundation methods and, above all, their impact on personnel. I think this "laugh and think" style could prove functional.
